---
title: "ClassicalShadow"
format: html
engine: julia
execute:
  daemon: false
julia:
  exeflags: ["--project=@."]
---

# AbstractShadow

## FactorizedShadow

### cases

```{julia}
using RandomMeas # MeasurementSettings
N = 2 
NU = 3 
measurement_settings = [LocalUnitaryMeasurementSetting(N) for _ in 1: NU];
site_indices = siteinds("Qubit",N);
ψ = random_mps(site_indices, linkdims=2); # Product Quantum state of interest
NM = 10 # Number of projective measurements per settings
measurement_group = MeasurementGroup(ψ , measurement_settings, NM);
```

```{julia}
classical_shadows = get_factorized_shadows(measurement_group); # Matrix{FactorizedShadow}  
size(classical_shadows) # the size is NU * NM, meaning every shot has a classical shadow.
println(classical_shadows[1, 1])
```

### docs

```{julia}
supertype(FactorizedShadow)
```

```{julia}
@doc FactorizedShadow
```

Fields

• shadow_data::Vector{ITensor}: A vector of ITensors (each 2×2) representing the shadow for each qubit/site.
• N::Int: Number of qubits/sites.
• site_indices::Vector{Index{Int64}}: A vector of site indices (length N).

In conclusion, the core part of Factorizedshadow is Vector{ITensor}, the actual operation with MPO that is shrinking is the indices of ITensor.

## DenseShadow

### cases

```{julia}
using RandomMeas 
N = 2 
NU = 3 
measurement_settings = [LocalUnitaryMeasurementSetting(N) for _ in 1: NU];
site_indices = siteinds("Qubit",N);
ψ = random_mps(site_indices, linkdims=2); 
NM = 10 
measurement_group = MeasurementGroup(ψ , measurement_settings, NM);
```

```{julia}
reduced_group = reduce_to_subsystem(measurement_group, [1, 2]) # Type: MeasurementGroup
dense_shadows = get_dense_shadows(reduced_group) # Vector{DenseShadow}
```

### doc

```{julia}
typeof(dense_shadows[1])
```

```{julia}
supertype(DenseShadow)
```

```{julia}
@doc DenseShadow
```


Fields
≡≡≡≡≡≡

•  shadow_data::ITensor: An ITensor with 2N indices representing the dense shadow.
•  N::Int: Number of sites (qubits).
•  site_indices::Vector{Index{Int64}}: A vector of site indices (length N).
